topics.title: Compliance Risk Profile (Solution Profile)
topics.description:
Description:
A Compliance Risk Profile, also known as a Solution Profile, is a structured assessment used to identify the compliance risks associated with a solution and determine the applicable IT compliance requirements. The profile helps ensure that solutions comply with legal, regulatory, corporate policy, and Pfizer Digital requirements throughout their lifecycle.
topics.content:
The Compliance Risk Profile process begins by creating a Solution Profile for a solution that is developed, supported, or managed by Pfizer Digital. Before profiling can start, the required deployment records must exist in the CMDB, and supplier/service records must be available if suppliers are responsible for any controls.
The Solution Profile Author creates the profile, associates the relevant solution and supplier records, and completes the profiling questionnaire. The questionnaire captures information about the solution's functionality, technology category, data usage, and compliance obligations. Based on the responses, the system determines the applicable compliance risk domains and generates the corresponding Compliance Requirements from the Common Requirements Set (CRS).
Once profiling is completed, the Solution Profile must be reviewed and confirmed by the appropriate stakeholders. Confirmation is required from the Solution Owner Representative and Digital Quality Assurance (BTQA). Additional reviewers such as Business Unit Quality Assurance (BUQA), Internal Controls Governance Council (ICGC), Privacy, Legal, or Security representatives may be required depending on the identified compliance domains.
After confirmation, the assigned Compliance Requirements must be evaluated to determine whether appropriate controls have been implemented. For each requirement, control information must be documented, including ownership, implementation status, control type, and control description. Requirements may be marked as Met, Met by Pfizer BT Service, Not Applicable, or Not Met. Any unmet requirement must either have a documented remediation plan or a formally approved risk acceptance.
The Solution Profile must be maintained throughout the solution lifecycle. Updates are required whenever significant changes occur, such as changes to functionality, data usage, compliance requirements, or remediation status. Even if no changes occur, the profile must be reviewed at least once every three years to ensure it remains accurate.
When a solution is retired or the profile is no longer required, the Solution Profile can be archived. Archived profiles remain available for record retention purposes but cannot be modified.
The completed Compliance Risk Profile provides a documented view of applicable compliance risks, assigned requirements, implemented controls, and stakeholder approvals, helping ensure ongoing compliance and effective risk management.
